pr_summary: Adds a user-lookup endpoint and an order-summary view. The feature works, but the lookup builds SQL via string concatenation (a critical injection risk) and the summary issues a query per order. Address the security issue before merging.
pr_intent: Add a user-lookup endpoint and an order-summary view.

findings.title: Possible SQL injection
findings.description: User-supplied `user_id` is concatenated directly into a SQL query string. An attacker can inject arbitrary SQL (e.g. `1 OR 1=1`) and read or modify other users' data.
findings.suggestion: Use a parameterized query instead of string concatenation.
findings.fix_diff:
- cursor.execute("SELECT * FROM users WHERE id=" + user_id)
+ cursor.execute("SELECT * FROM users WHERE id=%s", (user_id,))

findings.title: N+1 query inside loop
findings.description: A database query runs once per iteration over `orders`, turning one page load into N+1 round trips.
findings.suggestion: Fetch the related rows in a single query before the loop (e.g. a join or an `IN (...)` batch).

findings.title: Bare except swallows errors
findings.description: `except:` hides every error, including KeyboardInterrupt, and makes failures invisible.
findings.suggestion: Catch the specific exception you expect and log it.

findings.title: Missing function docstring
findings.description: `compute_total` has no docstring explaining its contract.
findings.suggestion: Add a one-line docstring describing inputs and return value.

critical_findings.title: Possible SQL injection
critical_findings.description: User-supplied `user_id` is concatenated directly into a SQL query string. An attacker can inject arbitrary SQL (e.g. `1 OR 1=1`) and read or modify other users' data.
critical_findings.suggestion: Use a parameterized query instead of string concatenation.
critical_findings.fix_diff:
- cursor.execute("SELECT * FROM users WHERE id=" + user_id)
+ cursor.execute("SELECT * FROM users WHERE id=%s", (user_id,))
